content.text: Project bootstrap and baseline architecture: Set up the MeridianOps Retail Performance platform with FastAPI backend, Vue.js frontend, PostgreSQL database, Docker Compose configuration, health endpoints, and initial project structure.

Bootstrapped the MeridianOps project with the following structure:

1. Backend (FastAPI):
   - app/main.py: FastAPI app factory with error handlers, CORS, request-ID middleware
   - app/core/config.py: Pydantic settings (DB URL, CORS, auth config)
   - app/api/router.py: API router aggregator
   - app/api/v1/endpoints/health.py: Liveness and database health probes
   - app/db/base.py: SQLAlchemy declarative base
   - app/db/session.py: Database session management
   - app/db/models/system.py: ServiceHeartbeat model
   - alembic/: Migration framework with initial baseline migration
   - Dockerfile: Multi-stage build (runtime + test targets)
   - pyproject.toml: Dependencies (fastapi, sqlalchemy, psycopg, bcrypt, etc.)

2. Frontend (Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite):
   - src/main.ts: App entry point
   - src/App.vue: Root component
   - src/router/index.ts: Vue Router configuration
   - src/stores/auth.ts: Pinia auth store
   - src/services/api.ts: Axios client with interceptors
   - src/views/: Login, Home, and system views
   - src/components/: Layout shell, navigation, common components
   - Dockerfile: Multi-stage build (deps + runtime + test)

3. Infrastructure:
   - docker-compose.yml: PostgreSQL, backend, frontend services with health checks
   - run_app.sh: Helper to start stack in detached mode
   - run_tests.sh: Sequential test runner (backend then frontend)

4. Documentation:
   - docs/design.md: Architecture overview
   - docs/api-spec.md: API endpoint catalog
   - repo/README.md: Docker workflow guide
